user_prompt: A user requested deletion of all their data. Generate the DSAR-deletion checklist including which systems to scrub.
actual_output:
## DSAR Deletion (Right to Erasure) Checklist — Art. 17

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Adapt to your specific tech stack and consult counsel for edge cases.*

### Phase 1: Intake & Validation (Day 1-3)

**1. Verify the request**
- [ ] Confirm requester identity (avoid social engineering — use existing account credentials or verified email; do not request excessive ID documentation per EDPB guidance)
- [ ] Log request in DSAR register with timestamp (response clock starts: **30 days**, extendable by 60 days for complex requests with notification)
- [ ] Send acknowledgment to user with reference number and expected timeline

**2. Assess legal grounds for refusal — Art. 17(3)**
Erasure can be refused or partially refused if data is needed for:
- [ ] Legal obligation compliance (e.g., tax records — typically 7-10 years)
- [ ] Establishment, exercise, or defense of legal claims
- [ ] Freedom of expression / public interest
- [ ] Active contractual obligations

If grounds for retention exist, document the lawful basis and inform the user which data will be retained and why.

### Phase 2: Systems Scrub Inventory

**Customer-facing systems**
- [ ] Production database — user record, profile, preferences
- [ ] Authentication system (Auth0, Cognito, Firebase, etc.)
- [ ] Application logs containing user identifiers
- [ ] File/object storage (S3, GCS) — uploads, avatars, documents
- [ ] Search indices (Elasticsearch, Algolia)
- [ ] Cache layers (Redis, Memcached)

**Analytics & tracking**
- [ ] Product analytics (Mixpanel, Amplitude, PostHog) — delete user profile
- [ ] Web analytics (GA4, Matomo) — submit user deletion request
- [ ] Session replay (Hotjar, FullStory, LogRocket)
- [ ] Heatmap tools
- [ ] A/B testing platforms (Optimizely, LaunchDarkly)

**Communication & marketing**
- [ ] Email service provider (Mailchimp, SendGrid, Customer.io) — suppress + delete
- [ ] CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive)
- [ ] Customer support (Zendesk, Intercom, Freshdesk) — tickets, chat history
- [ ] SMS/push notification services
- [ ] Marketing automation tools

**Operations & finance**
- [ ] Billing/payment processor (Stripe, Chargebee) — *retain transaction records per legal obligation, anonymize where possible*
- [ ] Invoicing system
- [ ] Tax/accounting software — *retain per local tax law*

**Backups & archives**
- [ ] Database backups — flag user ID for deletion on next backup rotation OR cryptographic deletion
- [ ] Archived logs in cold storage
- [ ] Document deletion-from-backups policy (EDPB accepts that backups can be addressed on rotation cycle if documented)

**Internal systems**
- [ ] Data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift)
- [ ] BI tools (Looker, Tableau) — cached dashboards
- [ ] Internal admin tools / Retool dashboards
- [ ] Slack/Teams channels referencing user (support escalations)

**Third-party processors — Art. 17(2) & 19**
- [ ] Notify all processors with copies of the user's data to delete
- [ ] Track confirmation receipts in DSAR log
- [ ] If data was made public, take reasonable steps to inform other controllers

### Phase 3: Special Considerations

**Derived data**
- [ ] ML model training data — if user data was used, document and assess whether model retraining is required (emerging area; document your reasoning)
- [ ] Aggregated/anonymized datasets — retain only if truly anonymized (no re-identification possible)
- [ ] Pseudonymized data linked to user — delete or fully anonymize

**Multi-account scenarios**
- [ ] Check if user has multiple accounts under different emails
- [ ] Joint accounts or referral relationships — delete only requester's data

**Audit trail**
- [ ] Retain a deletion log entry: user ID hash, request date, completion date, systems scrubbed (this log itself is lawful basis: legal claim defense)

### Phase 4: Confirmation & Closure

- [ ] Send written confirmation to user listing:
  - Data deleted
  - Data retained (with lawful basis and retention period)
  - Processors notified
  - Date of completion
- [ ] Update DSAR register: status = Completed
- [ ] If unable to fully comply, inform user of right to lodge complaint with supervisory authority

### Recommended SLA
| Step | Target |
|------|--------|
| Acknowledgment | 72 hours |
| Identity verification | Day 3-5 |
| Systems scrub | Day 5-25 |
| Confirmation to user | Day 25-30 |

### Red Flags Requiring Escalation
- Request involves a minor's data
- Active legal hold or litigation involves the user
- User is also an employee (different lawful bases apply)
- Data is in a system without deletion capability — document as a remediation gap

**Tip:** If you don't have one yet, build a `user_deletion()` orchestration script that hits each system's API in sequence. Manual deletion across 15+ tools is where most organizations fail the 30-day deadline.

Want me to generate template emails (acknowledgment, completion confirmation, partial-refusal explanation) or a DSAR register schema?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive, structured, and highly relevant checklist that directly answered the user's request while adhering to its persona.
